steps.title: Identify what the contrast requires
steps.content: To contrast two locations, the sentence must name both specimens, state both locations, and signal the difference.

steps.title: Match the winner to the requirement
steps.content:
D names both specimens and states both museum locations (Melbourne, Australia vs.

Ontario, Canada) with “while” signaling the contrast. Choices A, B, and C either cite only one location, emphasize similarity instead of contrast, or are too vague to establish a comparison.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
